The suspect, Rico Acevedo, was pronounced dead at the scene. Although officers believed he was armed, no firearm was recovered. He had charged at them while brandishing a tool that resembled a pistol. His criminal history included a 2015 domestic violence case involving his ex-girlfriend, where he was charged with domestic violence, felonious assault, aggravated burglary, aggravated robbery, and violating a protective order. Though he faced up to 40 years, he accepted a plea deal and served six. He was released in October 2021 and remained under state supervision at the time of his death.
